headline: Eversource Energy (ES) Stock Looks Undervalued Even As The Market Stays Cautious
summary: Eversource Energy has delivered a 19.1% return over the past year, yet valuation checks and an intrinsic value estimate based on a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) approach both point to the stock trading below what its cash flows suggest it could be worth. That combination of solid recent performance and an apparent valuation gap is drawing attention to whether the current price already reflects the company’s shift toward a pure-play regulated utility. Over the last 1 year, Eversource Energy...

headline: Did Eversource’s Aquarion Exit and Pure-Play Shift Just Redefine ES’s Risk-Reward Profile?
summary: Eversource Energy recently held its second-quarter 2026 earnings call, following the July 30 release of results, to brief analysts on its financial performance and business updates via a public webcast. The company’s repositioning as a pure-play regulated electric and natural gas utility, funded in part by the nearly US$1.70 billions Aquarion sale, highlights a sharper focus on core infrastructure and grid reliability investments. We’ll now examine how Eversource’s sharpened focus as a...

headline: Data Center Demand Has Investors Reevaluating U.S. Electric Utility Stocks
summary: The primary equity value distinction within the sector is between utilities with projected compound annual growth rates of 8% or greater and those with lower forecasts. Read more here.
